action.reasoning: Observation: Got LPIPS=0.6121 with avg elev/dist orbit rendering (n_views=11). Inspection shows render signature lacks azimuth param. Cameras have per-view azimuths that vary significantly while elevations are similar (~ -60 deg). Current approach doesn't match specific target poses. Analysis: The poor score is likely because the 11 rendered views are at default azimuth intervals starting from 0, not aligned to the specific target azimuths from cameras.json (and possibly input pose not canonicalized). Using relative azimuth = target_az - input_az + assumed_model_az (often 0) should align better. Getting source of render will reveal how to either pass azimuth or replicate camera setup for exact pose matching per target view. Decision: Update inspect_render.py to use inspect.getsource on render and renderer.render, test calling render with azimuth_deg param (both absolute and relative), then based on that we'll update predict.py to loop over each of the 11 targets and render with exact matching camera params. This should significantly lower LPIPS. Keep using pretrained TripoSR (no training yet). Test on val then submit_eval.
